titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/36

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=294 status=live modified=2021-07-20 12:11:18.52272+00 | translation moa=295 status=live modified=2021-04-06 11:31:18.310406+00

p.cl:
Omnibus Sancte matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Raderic MacGillescop . et Cristiana sponsa eius filia Rogeri
de Scalebroc Salutem .

Sciatis nos concessisse et hac nostra Carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie de Melr’ et monachis
ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam donationem Rogeri de Scalebroc quam fecit eidem
ecclesie et monachis predictis de piscatura de Don super harenam et una salina cum terris et toftis et Omnibus
aisiamentis que continentur in Carta predicti Rogeri tenenda et possidenda in perpetuum ita libere et quiete plenarie
et honorifice sicut aliqua elemosina liberius et quietius plenius et honorificentius tenetur uel possidetur et sicut Carta predicti
Rogeri testatur .

Et nos et heredes nostri manutenebimus et warrantizabimus eis omnia predicta et adquietabimus ea
erga omnes homines de omni seruitio in perpetuum .

Concessimus etiam eisdem monachis et hac Carta confirmauimus
totam terram de Drumteisuiene et de Alesburc et de Authenephur . quantum ad nos pertinet tenendam et possi
dendam in perpetuum ita libere et quiete et honorifice sicut carta predicti Rogeri et confirmatio domini nostri Dunecani . testan
tur .

Teste eodem Dunecano . Willelmo . vicecomite . de nouo Castello super Ar . Waltero clerico Regis Iohanne clerico . Iohanne fratre
Vdardi Gilleb’ mac Kenedi . Ewein mac Alewin . Gillenem maccolem’ .

Et quia sigillum proprium non
habuimus Dominus Dunecanus . rogatu nostro sigillum suum pro nobis huic carte nostre apposuit .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Rory mac Gillespie and Christiana his spouse, the daughter of Roger of Skelbrooke, send Greeting:

Know us to have granted, and by this our charter to have made firm, to God and to the Church of Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the donation of Roger of Skelbrooke which he made to the same church and monks aforesaid, of a fishery on the Doon above Harenam, and one saltpan, with lands and tofts and all easements that are contained in the charter of the aforesaid Roger, to be held and possessed for all time in this wise freely and quietly, fully and honourably, just as any alms is freely and quietly, fully and honourably held possessed, and just as the charter of the aforesaid Roger bears witness.
And we and our heirs shall maintain and warrant all things aforesaid to them and we shall acquit them in respect of all men from every service for all time.

We have also granted to the same monks, and by this charter have made firm, the whole land of Drumteisuiene and of Alesburc and of Authenephur, so much as belongs to us, to be held and possessed for all time, in this wise freely and quietly and honourably, just as the charter of the aforesaid Robert and the confirmation of our lord Duncan bear witness.

As witness, the same Duncan, William the sheriff of Newcastle-upon-Ayr, Walter the king’s clerk, John the clerk, John the brother of Udard, Gilbert mac Kennedy, Ewen mac Alewine, Gilla na Náem mac Colmán.
And since we did not have our own seal, the lord Duncan, at our request, has set his seal to this charter for us.
